pattern: Because both arrays are non-increasing, a two-pointer sweep that advances j greedily while nums1[i] <= nums2[j] never needs to backtrack i, giving the widest valid gap in linear time.
hints: A pair is valid only when i is at most j and the first value does not exceed the second.
Both arrays are sorted non-increasing, so once a j fails for some i it can only get worse for smaller i values.
Walk i and j forward together: while nums1[i] <= nums2[j] keep pushing j and record j - i, otherwise advance i.

edge_inputs.description: best pair sits in the middle
flat second array allows a wide gap
single matching element gives distance zero

stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n1 = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    n2 = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    a = sorted((rng.randint(1, 100000) for _ in range(n1)), reverse=True)
    b = sorted((rng.randint(1, 100000) for _ in range(n2)), reverse=True)
    return [a, b]
